brief_justification: Paper A is a measure-theoretic and Monte Carlo analysis of a deterministic billiard dynamical system, characterizing the set of clearing strokes as a positive-Lebesgue-measure event and estimating rare-event probabilities P(k); Paper B is a neural-network approximation theory for nonlinear frequency alignment in mixed-frequency forecasting, with a non-asymptotic prediction error bound. The only overlap is generic use of simulation studies, not a shared governing process or role-for-role structure, so no coherent mapping holds.
